expectations.text: Identifies the work as belonging to the Deployment & Domains area of the platform
expectations.evidence: Line 5: names the work as spanning Backend Services, Deployment & Domains, and Celery & Background Workers.

expectations.text: Recommends modeling the flow as a resumable state machine that commits each transition (Principle 16), not one blocking function
expectations.evidence: Dedicated 'Principle 16' section (lines 9-15) and line 34: 'Replace the one blocking finalize_domain ... with a small resumable state machine.'

expectations.text: Recommends the domain move through explicit persisted intermediate states that can resume after a crash
expectations.evidence: Step 1 (line 36): define explicit states dns_verified -> gsc_pending -> gsc_verified -> active, each transition committed so a crash leaves recoverable state.

expectations.text: Recommends running the slow external GSC verification work asynchronously off the request path (Principle 19)
expectations.evidence: 'Principle 19' section (lines 17-19) and step 3 (line 45): GSC poll runs as a Celery task; route returns immediately; client polls.

expectations.text: Recommends bounding the verification retries with backoff and an explicit terminal state (Principle 21)
expectations.evidence: 'Principle 21' section (lines 21-23): finite, backed-off retry terminating in retry_exhausted.

expectations.text: Explicitly advises against the user's proposed single blocking finalize_domain function as written
expectations.evidence: Line 3 and line 34 explicitly say the single-function plan does not fit and should be replaced.

expectations.text: Grounds the advice in the appsmith-v2 principles catalogue and cites specific principle numbers
expectations.evidence: Cites P16, P19, P21, P15, P17, P2, P6, P20, P5, P8, P37 and quotes the contract throughout.
